key_insight: The paper reframes high-order finite elements so the cell-wise vector becomes the persistent data model, enabling exact Krylov solves on unassembled data with communication pushed into the preconditioner.
engineering_relevance: This can improve GPU finite-element solvers by reducing assembly overhead and making memory layout a tunable performance parameter for complex meshes.
method: Uses flexible conjugate gradients directly on cell-wise storage with a primal-dual identity to match assembled-solve scalars.
Implements direct stiffness summation via dimensionally split face-exchange sweeps instead of gather-scatter, atomics, or coloring.
Handles unstructured macro-block interfaces and h-adaptive hanging nodes with separate topological kernels and a shadow-cell wrapper.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on modern GPUs showing operator evaluation and solver performance that exceed state-of-the-art matrix-free implementations.
watch_item: Performance depends on preconditioners with continuous image and on careful GPU memory-layout choices, which may complicate integration for general meshes.
paper_title: Coalesced Matrix-Free Finite Elements in Cell-Wise Storage
